title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A new photo has been released in the unsolved 1997 murder of Rosalie Miller in Auburn, New Hampshire.
Miller was found dead from ligature strangulation in 1997; last seen in Manchester, NH, in December 1996.
Authorities seek help from anyone who saw or spoke with Miller in December 1996 near Londonderry Turnpike.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, charged with murder after allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island.
Sousa allegedly caused a fatal car crash in Swansea, Massachusetts, killing two people shortly after the shooting.
Police found a pistol and ammunition in Sousa's car; he is in custody at Rhode Island Hospital.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins eye Robert Thomas from St. Louis Blues, requiring several draft picks and prospects for a trade.
Thomas, 26, has exceeded 80 points in consecutive seasons. He is considered a top-six center prospect.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney emphasizes caution in trades but remains open to enhancing the team before the deadline.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett agreed to a $3,000 fine without admitting guilt for tree poisoning in Rockport, Maine.
The Maine Board of Pesticides Control approved the settlement despite lacking an apology from the couple for the tree deaths.
The late Ruth Graham's son proposed the couple make a donation for coastal preservation, a request not addressed by them.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: KC Concepcion and Omar Cooper are potential wide receiver targets for the Patriots in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Edge rushers Romello Height, Dani Dennis-Sutton, and Malachi Lawrence impressed with strong combine performances.
Max Iheanachor, an offensive tackle from Arizona State, remains a viable first-round pick option for the Patriots.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots unlikely to trade major draft picks for a star player this offseason, per ESPN's Mike Reiss.
New England reportedly interested in Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby but not at high draft cost.
Patriots focus on building through the draft, with 11 picks in 2026, mostly in the later rounds.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power receives state approval to build Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage facility in Everett.
The facility will occupy 16.5 acres of the former ExxonMobil tank farm, featuring 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures.
The wider Docklands project includes 3.3 million sq ft of lab and office space and 3,200 residential units.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'SNL' on Feb. 28 with Mumford & Sons as the musical guest for the show's 51st season.
Storrie's 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams appeared in a skating sketch and helped introduce the musical guest.
'SNL' featured appearances by US Olympic hockey medalists, with Hillary Knight addressing President Trump's remarks.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 1-0 to New York Red Bulls at Sports Illustrated Stadium, marking their second defeat of the season.
Revolution's improved defense, featuring Brayan Ceballos and Mamadou Fofana, withstood intense first-half pressure but struggled in possession.
Despite a second-half opportunity, New England failed to convert chances, with Red Bulls' press disrupting their attacking rhythm.

title: Triston Casas reportedly unlikely to be ready by Opening Day, while Kutter Crawford, Patrick Sandoval take step forward in rehab
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Triston Casas is unlikely to be ready for Opening Day due to a torn patellar tendon sustained last May.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval recently threw live batting practice as they work toward possible Opening Day availability.
Red Sox roster moves create uncertainty for Casas, Crawford, and Sandoval's roles due to acquisitions and roster competition.
